A.error_quality: free/typed - two-variant suberror BankError (NonPositiveAmount, InsufficientFunds) via the typed raise effect; only a non-fatal derive(Show) deprecation warning. No fix needed (pass@1).
A.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written guards. balance is a plain `mut Int`; nothing structural prevents negatives. deposit/withdraw raise before mutating (amount<=0; amount>balance), so balance stays >=0 and unchanged on error. The type system forces error handling (raise BankError effect must be discharged) but does not prove non-negativity.
A.toolchain_notes: moon 0.1.20260529. Scaffold `moon new bankaccount` (KDL-style moon.mod/moon.pkg). 14 blackbox tests in bankaccount_test.mbt. Run: cd bankaccount && moon test. NO guide consulted; syntax from prior knowledge.

B.error_quality: structured - suberror AccountError with NonPositiveAmount(Int)/InsufficientFunds(balance, requested) via raise; error [3016] named the exact labelled-arg syntax fix.
B.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written guards. balance is a plain `mut Int` (no refinement/contract). guard clauses reject amount<=0 and amount>balance, raising before any mutation; only subtraction is gated, so balance never goes <0 and is unchanged on error. Compiler does not prove the invariant.
raw_error_excerpts: Error [3016] The syntax `balance~=..` for supplying labelled argument is invalid, use `balance=..`
